situation.headline: Active US-Iran military exchange underway: US striking Iranian nuclear, naval, and port infrastructure while Iran launches ballistic missiles at US bases in Iraq, Jordan, and Gulf partners, with Bushehr nuclear plant hit and Strait of Hormuz access now in active contest

domains.name: Middle East
domains.label: regional escalation 90d
domains.status: Direct US-Iran kinetic conflict is live, with Iranian missiles hitting US facilities in Bahrain and Iraq, US striking Bushehr, Chabahar, Bandar Abbas, and IRGC Navy assets, and Gulf partners Jordan and Kuwait already under fire — regional war contagion risk is not hypothetical, it is occurring.

domains.name: Indo-Pacific
domains.label: China coercion escalation
domains.status: US military assets and attention are being consumed by the Iran theater, creating an opportunistic window Beijing is almost certainly war-gaming; no direct triggering event in context but distraction risk is high.

domains.name: Europe
domains.label: NATO cohesion fracture
domains.status: Putin is rejecting negotiation and likely escalating in Ukraine while the US is operationally engaged in the Gulf, compressing NATO bandwidth, but no immediate cohesion-breaking event is evident in the current data.

domains.name: Energy
domains.label: supply shock 60d
domains.status: US strikes on Chabahar, Bandar Abbas, and Bushehr plus Iranian missile fire across the Gulf directly threaten Strait of Hormuz transit carrying roughly 20 percent of global oil; a supply shock in 60 days is near-certain if kinetics persist.

domains.name: US Politics
domains.label: House flip Nov 2026
domains.status: Kalshi prediction markets show Rubio at 31 percent for GOP nomination and AOC at 18 percent Democrat, signaling party-base polarization; war rally effect may temporarily boost Trump, but economic fallout from an energy shock and the IS TRUMP ON AMBIEN narrative suggest vulnerability — markets pricing Dem gains.

situation.unseen: The simultaneous US strikes on Chabahar Port and the Iran-Turkmenistan railway bridge are not just tactical anti-IRGC moves — they functionally sever Iran's overland and maritime eastern logistics corridors, which are also the routes China uses for Belt and Road connectivity to the Gulf; Beijing's economic exposure to this conflict is being systematically underweighted by analysts focused on the Hormuz oil lane alone.
situation.criticalWindow: The next 72 hours around any Iranian strike on or near Bushehr nuclear infrastructure combined with Iranian ballistic missiles already hitting US bases in Bahrain and Iraq — if a reactor event or US personnel mass casualty occurs, the political threshold for a qualitative escalation to strikes on Iranian leadership or nuclear program itself collapses, and the 30-day window for any diplomatic off-ramp closes permanently.
watchFor: Confirmed US personnel killed in Bahrain or Baghdad base strikes, triggering mandatory presidential escalation response under Rules of Engagement
Iranian closure declaration or mining of Strait of Hormuz chokepoint, converting energy supply shock from probable to certain
Chinese naval or air activity in South China Sea or Taiwan Strait exploiting US CENTCOM preoccupation and carrier repositioning

Qatari mediation collapse — Qatari PM already calling Iranian FM to condemn tanker attacks, meaning the one functional back-channel is under strain and its failure removes the last diplomatic circuit-breaker
situation.confidenceNote: The OSINT feed is high-volume and unverified; specific casualty figures, exact strike coordinates, and damage assessments for Bushehr and Bahrain US HQ should be treated as provisional until CENTCOM official statements confirm. Prediction-market data reflects US-based retail sentiment and may lag rapidly evolving kinetic developments by hours. The Drudge headline Trump abandoning the Qatari aircraft amid threats introduces an unresolved personal security variable for the US head of state that cannot be assessed without classified detail.
situation.topPattern: The US is executing a deliberate infrastructure-degradation campaign against Iran's naval and nuclear logistics nodes — Chabahar, Bandar Abbas, Bushehr, Kerman, Kermanshah — while Iran is retaliating not against US homeland or Israel but specifically against Gulf-based US force projection hubs in Bahrain, Iraq, and Jordan, suggesting Iran is attempting to raise the cost of basing rights for Gulf partners rather than directly escalate with the US homeland; Kalshi's Rubio 31 percent Republican nomination price ticking up 2 cents in 24 hours while Trump resignation sits at 21 percent OI suggests markets are already faintly pricing a leadership continuity risk premium into the 2028 race concurrent with the shooting war.

signals.headline: Iran conducts sustained ballistic and cruise missile strikes on US bases and regional allies; US responds with multi-day air campaign
signals.summary: Iranian military launched coordinated missile attacks from multiple provinces (Urmia, Arak, northwestern bases) targeting Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan, US Camp Victoria in Baghdad, and US Consulate in Erbil. US Central Command executed follow-on strikes across five Iranian provinces including Bushehr nuclear facility, Chabahar Port, and rail infrastructure. At least 14 Iranian killed, 78 wounded. Regional air defenses activated across Jordan, Kuwait, Bahrain.
signals.signal: This is active armed escalation between US and Iran—not a measured regional crisis. Sustained two-way strike pattern indicates either deliberate de-escalation ceiling has been breached or both sides are signaling red-line boundaries. Nuclear facility strike (even if targeting IRGC assets nearby) raises nuclear incident risk. No Trump administration statement in context suggests unclear US command continuity messaging.
signals.contradicts: Market pricing shows zero movement on Iran war risk or energy prices despite active kinetic exchange. Prediction markets flat on Trump resignation (21%) despite operational chaos signals.

signals.headline: Qatar withdraws from mediation role; condemns tanker attacks amid US-Iran escalation
signals.summary: Qatari Prime Minister condemned tanker attacks in call with Iranian Foreign Minister. Drudge headline references Trump abandoning Qatari-One (diplomatic mediation track). Qatar signals it is stepping back from US-brokered regional stabilization efforts.
signals.signal: Loss of Qatar as neutral broker collapses back-channel diplomacy infrastructure. This removes a key off-ramp mechanism during active kinetic phase. Suggests regional actors expect extended confrontation, not rapid negotiation.
signals.contradicts: No Kalshi market reflects diplomatic breakdown or regional stability degradation.

signals.headline: Prediction markets show Marco Rubio 31% Republican nominee odds; up 2¢ in 24h; Todd Blanche Trump Attorney General at 78%
signals.summary: Rubio trending in GOP nomination markets despite current Trump administration. Blanche locked in as next AG at 78% odds. Warnock (1% Dem nominee) fueling 2028 speculation per Drudge. Market structure suggests Trump trajectory uncertainty in medium term.
signals.signal: Market-implied succession planning (Rubio, Blanche continuity) suggests traders price in Trump administration transition risk or term limit boundary. Blanche AG pricing at 78% indicates expectation of immediate cabinet turnover, potentially signaling anticipated legal/political pressure on sitting administration.
signals.contradicts: Trump resignation market at only 21% despite active Iran war, legal headwinds, and cabinet volatility signals in markets.

signals.headline: Zitron calls for blocking OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs; warns investors being conned by AI hype
signals.summary: Prominent AI/semiconductor analyst Zitron publicly demands regulatory prohibition on major AI lab IPOs, citing investor fraud risk. Drudge headline emphasizes conning narrative. Reflects growing skepticism from insider technical authority.
signals.signal: If Zitron's critique gains regulatory traction, it signals potential policy shift to treat frontier AI labs as strategic infrastructure (not IPO-able businesses). This could redirect capital flows and alter US competitiveness posture vs. China. Markets show zero reaction—complacency or suppression of information.
signals.contradicts: No corresponding Kalshi market movement on AI regulation; no tech stock selloff in context.

signals.headline: US strikes Iranian Chabahar Port and Bushehr Nuclear Facility; rail infrastructure destroyed; tanker attacks ongoing
signals.summary: US operations targeted Iran's primary Arabian Sea port (Chabahar), IRGC Navy base at Bushehr, and Aq Tekeh Khan Bridge on Iran-Turkmenistan rail corridor. Iranian state media reports cruise missile impact on Bushehr nuclear plant site. Ongoing tanker attack reports in Strait of Hormuz.
signals.signal: Destruction of Chabahar Port and rail links to Turkmenistan signals intent to sever Iran's non-Gulf economic corridors and isolate it from Central Asia. Nuclear facility strike (even if precision) creates latent escalation hazard. Strait of Hormuz tanker attacks imply energy supply risk. Oil markets should be pricing significant risk premium; context shows no corresponding energy futures movement.
signals.contradicts: Energy markets appear insensitive to active Hormuz insecurity and port destruction. Suggests either markets are downstream-hedged or information lag.

signals.headline: Greenland acquisition market prices No Acquisition at 83%; up 1¢ in 24h despite Trump statements
signals.summary: Kalshi market on US Greenland acquisition during Trump term shows 83% probability of no acquisition. Market has ticked up slightly (1¢) in recent 24 hours despite Trump's public interest in Greenland purchase.
signals.signal: Market confidence that Greenland acquisition will not occur during Trump term is very high and hardening. This suggests traders assess either (a) Danish/Greenlandic legal/political barriers as insurmountable, (b) Trump's stated interest as theater, or (c) Trump term may not span full negotiation timeline. Contradicts Trump's apparent public push.
signals.contradicts: Trump's recent public statements emphasizing Greenland acquisition interest appear divorced from market pricing of likelihood.

scanNote: Context is dominated by active Iran-US kinetic exchange and domestic political market positioning; energy and financial markets show anomalous non-reaction to geopolitical shocks, suggesting either information lag or cross-asset hedging opacity.

markets.label: Trump resigns before term ends
markets.rationale: Drudge headline questioning Trump's cognitive state ('IS TRUMP ON AMBIEN?') and the abandonment of the Qatari aircraft signal domestic political stress, but resignation remains a very low-probability outcome historically and no credible intel supports an imminent departure.

markets.label: No U.S. acquisition of Greenland this term
markets.rationale: With CENTCOM fully consumed by active strikes against Iran across at least five provinces, the Gulf, and Iraq, no bandwidth exists for the diplomatic or coercive effort required to advance Greenland acquisition; market at 83% is slightly underpriced relative to this operational reality.

markets.label: Todd Blanche next Attorney General
markets.rationale: Drudge headline on Dems gaining a Republican ally in a probe of Patel's FBI spending implies Patel's tenure is under pressure, making a reshuffle possible, but Blanche at 78% in the market seems slightly high given the political turbulence created by the Iran conflict dominating executive attention.

markets.label: Marco Rubio 2028 GOP nominee
markets.rationale: Rubio is at 31% on Kalshi and megadonor Ken Griffin's stated backing is a meaningful signal, but his visibility as a hawkish Secretary of State during an active US-Iran war could either elevate or damage him depending on how the conflict resolves before 2028.

markets.label: Gavin Newsom 2028 Dem nominee
markets.rationale: Newsom leads the Dem field at 20% on Kalshi, but the war footing and falling public support for capitalism per Drudge polling could advantage a more left-populist candidate; his California brand is a structural liability in a wartime cycle.

markets.label: AOC 2028 Dem nominee
markets.rationale: At 18% on Kalshi, AOC benefits from anti-war sentiment and the poll showing falling support for capitalism, but active US strikes on Iran may generate rally-around-the-flag dynamics that suppress an antiwar candidacy's ceiling through 2028.

markets.label: US macro distress composite triggers (2+ conditions)
markets.rationale: Active US-Iran hostilities with strikes on Chabahar Port, Bushehr, and Bandar Abbas threaten Strait of Hormuz shipping and energy prices, materially elevating the probability of an oil shock, equity correction, and labor market deterioration that could trigger multiple conditions in this basket by July 2028.

markets.label: J.D. Vance wins 2028 presidential election
markets.rationale: As sitting Vice President during an active military campaign against Iran, Vance inherits incumbency advantages and hawkish credibility, but a prolonged or costly war could equally undermine the administration's 2028 prospects; 17% market price is approximately fair.

watchSignals: Iranian military posture and retaliation timescale following US strikes on Bushehr, Chabahar, and five provinces—watch for escalation or de-escalation signals within 72 hours
US base vulnerability assessment across Iraq, Kuwait, Bahrain after confirmed missile impacts on Camp Victoria and CENTCOM HQ—indicators of IRGC operational competence
Qatar diplomatic status and mediation role post-strike; Qatari PM phone call with Iranian FM suggests potential off-ramp but Trump abandoning Qatari-One raises US posture questions
Russian weapons deliveries to Iran or proxy forces as retaliation coordination mechanism—watch HUMINT on UAC production and routing through Syria/Iraq
Israeli air defense integration and potential secondary strike coordination with US—Greek F-16 fire incident may signal broader regional air ops tempo
Domestic political reaction: Trump administration consistency on Iran escalation vs. Rubio nomination signal (31% market price, +2¢ 24h)—watch for Cabinet coherence messaging

groundTruth.headline: US conducted multi-site airstrike campaign against Iran July 8-9: Bushehr IRGC Navy base, Chabahar Port, Aq Tekeh Khan Bridge (Iran-Turkmenistan railway), plus strikes in Tabriz, Bandar Abbas, Kerman, Kermanshah
groundTruth.summary: Largest US kinetic action against Iranian targets in 2 years. Iran reports 14 KIA, 78 WIA across five provinces. US explicitly degrading commercial shipping interdiction capability and port infrastructure. Strike scope indicates pre-planned, multi-objective operation.
groundTruth.signal: US executing containment strategy on Iranian naval/maritime threat; major escalation from coercive diplomacy baseline; creates reciprocal strike expectation within 1-2 weeks

groundTruth.headline: Iran launched ballistic and cruise missiles from Urmia, Arak toward US-allied targets: Muwaffaq Salti AB (Jordan), Bushehr nuclear site, with 8+ missiles intercepted by Jordan, 3+1+10 intercepted by Kuwait, Bahrain sirens activated
groundTruth.summary: Iranian military conducted dispersed, multi-axis missile barrage. Partial interception by allies suggests some leakage but no confirmed US casualty reports yet. Nuclear power plant strike claim (Bushehr) is escalatory rhetoric but unconfirmed damage.
groundTruth.signal: Iran demonstrated willingness to strike US infrastructure directly and escalate to nuclear targets rhetorically; US air defense posture holding but stressed; potential for miscalculation or US secondary response

groundTruth.headline: Multiple US base complexes (Camp Victoria Baghdad, CENTCOM HQ Bahrain, US Consulate Erbil) report sirens and missile impacts; no major casualty reports but material damage confirmed in Kuwait and Bahrain
groundTruth.summary: US forward-deployed force under direct Iranian kinetic threat. Interception rates held but psychological and operational strain evident. Regional allies (Jordan, Kuwait, Bahrain) activated air defenses, confirming threat credibility.
groundTruth.signal: US force posture in Gulf degraded temporarily; ally confidence in US air defense and rapid response capability under test; potential draw-down of civilian personnel or force repositioning expected

groundTruth.headline: Qatar PM engaged in de-escalation call with Iranian FM on tanker attacks; simultaneously Trump abandoning Qatari-One facility suggests US reducing confidence in Qatar mediation or signaling independent US action
groundTruth.summary: Qatar historically served as back-channel for US-Iran talks. Qatari outreach post-strike suggests active mediation attempt, but Trump's Qatari facility withdrawal contradicts traditional diplomatic leverage patterns.
groundTruth.signal: Confusion in Trump admin messaging on Qatar role; possible independent Trump action bypassing traditional diplomatic channels; Qatar reliability as mediator in doubt

groundTruth.headline: Prediction market shows Marco Rubio (Secretary of State nominee) at 31% for 2028 GOP nomination (+2¢ 24h move), while Trump himself at 3%, J.D. Vance at 17%, Ron DeSantis at 3% for GOP nomination
groundTruth.summary: Market pricing shows significant uncertainty on Trump 2028 availability. Rubio emergence as front-runner suggests markets see Rubio as post-Trump continuity candidate. High open interest (2.3M) confirms deep engagement.
groundTruth.signal: Markets not pricing Trump 2028 comeback as lock; foreign policy hawks (Rubio) gaining positioning; potential for GOP realignment if Trump incapacitated or term ends badly on Iran/Middle East
